summary: Comprehensive testing of FURNI e-commerce platform completed. All 17 backend API tests passed (100%). All frontend UI tests passed including login, dashboard, orders CRUD, and CSV import functionality.

minor.issue: Console shows past 500 errors due to invalid date data in DB - this appears to be cleaned up now as current tests pass

issues: Console warning: HTML hydration error - <span> wrapping <tr> inside <tbody>. This is a React warning but doesn't affect functionality.

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: Orders.js line 246-248 - React hydration warning about <span> inside <tbody>. Consider removing wrapper span when mapping orders in JSX.
seed_data_creation: Test orders created with TEST_ prefix were cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core features working: Auth (login/register), Dashboard (stats, recent orders with proper date handling), Orders (CRUD, filtering by status/channel, search), CSV/TXT import. WhatsApp integration requires actual Meta App setup - not tested. Console shows React hydration warnings for Orders table but functionality works correctly.

features_tested.user_registration_and_login: PASS - Login with test@furni.com works, invalid credentials properly rejected
features_tested.dashboard_stats_display: PASS - All 8 stat cards display correctly without crashing
features_tested.dashboard_recent_orders: PASS - Orders display with proper date formatting, N/A shown for invalid dates
features_tested.order_creation_via_form: PASS - Form submission works, order appears in list with success toast
features_tested.orders_list_page: PASS - All orders display with dates formatted correctly
features_tested.amazon_txt_import: PASS - Backend accepts .txt files with tab-separated data
features_tested.import_modal_csv_txt: PASS - File input accepts both .csv and .txt files
features_tested.orders_filtering: PASS - Status and channel filters work correctly

rca_of_issue: Previous 500 errors on /api/orders/ were caused by orders with empty string '' for order_date and dispatch_by fields. These have been cleaned from database. Frontend now has validation at line 165 of Dashboard.js and line 286 of Orders.js to handle invalid dates gracefully.
